title: IRS Code 971 AC 065 Meaning (Innocent Spouse Relief Pending) – For Tax Professionals
description: IRS Code 971 with Action Code 065 indicates an Innocent Spouse relief request is pending/under consideration. Learn what it signals, what it affects, and what to document next.
content:
IRS Transcript Codes • Built for 750,000+ U.S. Tax Professionals

IRS Code 971 AC 065 Meaning

IRS Code 971 is a miscellaneous notice/action entry. The meaning depends on the Action Code. When you see 971 AC 065, it generally indicates an Innocent Spouse relief request (Form 8857) is pending or under consideration for that module. This page shows how to read it in context, what it usually affects, and what to document for next steps.

Try the parser (requires credits)

What is IRS Code 971 AC 065?

Plain-English definition: IRS Code 971 is a general bucket for notices and miscellaneous module actions. When paired with Action Code 065, it typically indicates the IRS recorded an Innocent Spouse relief claim (Form 8857) that is pending or under consideration for that tax module.

Common transcript type
Account Transcript
Usually appears near
Codes 570, 972, 971
Typical context
Innocent Spouse claim tracking
Client-friendly summary
"The IRS is reviewing an Innocent Spouse request"

What does it usually affect?

  • Collection planning: It’s a signal to slow down and verify what collection actions are active on the module.
  • Client communications: You can explain that a relief request exists, but avoid promising outcomes before the IRS decision.
  • Documentation: It’s a cue to organize facts, timelines, and supporting records aligned to Form 8857 and the tax years involved.

What should a tax professional do next?

  1. Confirm the module: Identify the exact tax period and liability the 971 AC 065 is attached to.
  2. Check surrounding activity: Read entries before and after the 971 line to see freezes, notices, or reversals.
  3. Document the claim: Note Form 8857 filing date, basis, and supporting facts. Store a clean client-facing summary.

Example: how this looks in a parsed report

Below is a simplified view of how 971 AC 065 may appear in a client-ready timeline:

Innocent Spouse indicator
Code 971 AC 065 – Innocent Spouse relief claim recorded
Date: 04/10/2024
Code 570 – Additional account action pending
Date: 04/15/2024
Code 972 – Reversal of a 971 entry
Date: 06/03/2024

FAQ

What does IRS Code 971 AC 065 mean on a transcript?

It typically indicates the IRS recorded an Innocent Spouse relief request (Form 8857) and the claim is pending/under consideration for that tax module.

Does 971 AC 065 stop collections?

Not automatically. Confirm current collection status, any freezes, and enforcement activity before advising next steps.

How do I explain this to a client?

Explain that the IRS has logged the relief request and is reviewing it. Don’t promise relief until you see outcome activity and/or written determination.

What other codes should I check?

Review 570, 971 entries around the timeline, and 972 reversals to understand what changed and when.
